Title: Create a patient with linked person, name and identifier via POST /patient
Preconditions: Valid session token; an identifier type and identifier source configured; a person payload available
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate and obtain session | 2. POST /ws/rest/v1/patient with person and identifiers arrays | 3. Capture the returned uuid | 4. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 with patient uuid; subsequent GET returns the patient with the identifier marked preferred and matching person demographics

Title: Reject patient creation with missing required gender (400)
Preconditions: Valid session token
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/patient with a person lacking gender | 2. Inspect status and error body
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation error message naming the missing required gender field; no patient is created

Title: Reject patient creation with duplicate preferred identifier (409/400)
Preconditions: An existing patient already holds identifier 1000X in the same identifier source
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/patient reusing identifier 1000X with a unique-enforcing identifier type | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Request is rejected with HTTP 400 or 409 and a uniqueness violation message; no duplicate patient is created

Title: Update patient identifier via POST /patient/{uuid}/identifier/{id}
Preconditions: An existing patient with at least one identifier
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?v=full to read identifier uuid | 2. POST /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}/identifier/{idUuid} with a new identifier value | 3. Re-GET the patient
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; the identifier subresource reflects the new value; audit metadata (dateChanged, changedBy) is updated

Title: Void (soft-delete) a patient via DELETE /patient/{uuid} with reason
Preconditions: An existing non-voided patient with no blocking dependencies
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?reason=TestVoid | 2. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} | 3. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?includeAll=true
Expected_Result: DELETE returns 204; plain GET returns 404 (patient hidden); GET with includeAll=true returns the patient with voided=true and voidReason populated

Title: Purge a patient via DELETE with purge=true (hard delete)
Preconditions: A test patient with no clinical data referencing it; superuser privileges
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?purge=true | 2. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?includeAll=true
Expected_Result: DELETE returns 204; the patient cannot be retrieved even with includeAll=true (record physically removed)

Title: Purge fails with 500/409 when patient has dependent clinical data
Preconditions: A patient that has at least one encounter or observation
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid}?purge=true | 2. Inspect status
Test_Data: purge=true on a patient with encounters
Expected_Result: Purge is rejected with a constraint error (HTTP 500 or 409); the patient remains retrievable and unmodified

Title: Compare patient ref vs default vs full representation field sets
Preconditions: An existing patient with names, identifiers and attributes
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient/{uuid}?v=ref | 2. GET /patient/{uuid}?v=default | 3. GET /patient/{uuid}?v=full | 4. Diff the JSON keys
Expected_Result: ref returns only uuid, display and links; default adds identifiers and person summary; full adds auditInfo, attributes and expanded subresources; each level is a strict superset

Title: Custom representation selects only requested fields via v=custom
Preconditions: An existing patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient/{uuid}?v=custom:(uuid,person:(gender,age)) | 2. Inspect returned keys
Expected_Result: Response contains exactly uuid and a person object with only gender and age; no other fields are present

Title: Malformed custom representation returns 400
Preconditions: An existing patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient/{uuid}?v=custom:(uuid,person:(gender | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a representation parse error; no partial data leaked

Title: Create a person with multiple names and addresses via POST /person
Preconditions: Valid session token
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/person with two names and two addresses | 2. GET /person/{uuid}?v=full
Test_Data: names: preferred + alias; addresses: home + work
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; full GET returns both names with exactly one preferred=true, and both addresses with correct address fields

Title: Set and read a person attribute via /person/{uuid}/attribute
Preconditions: An existing person and a configured person attribute type (e.g. Telephone)
Test_Steps: 1. POST /person/{uuid}/attribute with attributeType and value | 2. GET /person/{uuid}/attribute
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 then 200; the attribute is listed with correct type and value and is not voided

Title: Birthdate boundary: future birthdate is rejected
Preconditions: Valid session token; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. POST /person with birthdate one day in the future | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation message; person is not created (birthdate cannot be in the future)

Title: Boundary: birthdate exactly today is accepted
Preconditions: Valid session token; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. POST /person with birthdate equal to today | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; person created with age 0; today is an inclusive valid boundary

Title: Update gender via POST /person/{uuid} retains other fields
Preconditions: An existing person with gender F
Test_Steps: 1. POST /person/{uuid} with gender=M only | 2. GET /person/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; gender updated to M; names, addresses and attributes remain unchanged; auditInfo.dateChanged updated

Title: Create an encounter with obs and provider via POST /encounter
Preconditions: An existing patient with an active visit; an encounter type, location, form and encounter role configured
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/encounter with patient, encounterType, location, encounterDatetime, encounterProviders and obs | 2. GET /encounter/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; full GET shows the encounter linked to the visit with the provider in the correct role and the obs persisted

Title: Reject encounter with encounterDatetime before visit start (400)
Preconditions: A patient with an active visit started today
Test_Steps: 1. POST /encounter with encounterDatetime one day before visit startDatetime | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation error; encounter must fall within the visit window and is not created

Title: Reject encounter with future encounterDatetime (400)
Preconditions: Valid session; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. POST /encounter with encounterDatetime in the future | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; encounter datetime cannot be in the future; not created

Title: Void an encounter cascades void to its observations
Preconditions: An encounter with at least two obs, not voided
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /encounter/{uuid}?reason=Correction | 2. GET /encounter/{uuid}?includeAll=true | 3. Inspect each obs voided flag
Expected_Result: DELETE returns 204; encounter voided=true; all child obs are also voided=true with the same void reason (cascade)

Title: Query encounters by patient and encounterType with paging
Preconditions: A patient with more than 5 encounters of a given type
Test_Steps: 1. GET /encounter?patient={uuid}&encounterType={uuid}&limit=2&startIndex=0 | 2. GET next page with startIndex=2
Expected_Result: Each page returns at most 2 encounters; results are non-overlapping across pages; a links.next is present when more exist

Title: Create a numeric observation within concept reference range
Preconditions: An existing encounter and a numeric concept (e.g. Weight kg) with a defined range
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/obs with person, concept, value and obsDatetime linked to the encounter | 2. GET /obs/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; obs persisted with numeric value 70 and correct units; linked to encounter and person

Title: Numeric obs above absolute high boundary is rejected or flagged
Preconditions: A numeric concept Weight with absoluteHigh=500 kg
Test_Steps: 1. POST /obs with value above absoluteHigh | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 (or 200 with a critical-high flag per configuration); value beyond absolute range is not silently accepted as normal

Title: Coded obs rejects a value not in the concept answer set
Preconditions: A coded concept with a fixed answer set
Test_Steps: 1. POST /obs with a value concept uuid outside the answer set | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; the coded answer is not a permitted answer for the question concept; obs not created

Title: Obs are immutable: editing creates a new version and voids old
Preconditions: An existing non-voided obs
Test_Steps: 1. POST /obs/{uuid} with a changed value | 2. GET the original uuid?includeAll=true | 3. GET the new obs
Expected_Result: Original obs becomes voided with a new replacement obs created; history is preserved (immutability honored)

Title: Group obs (obsGroup) creates parent-child structure
Preconditions: A convSet/grouping concept configured
Test_Steps: 1. POST /obs with groupMembers array under a grouping concept | 2. GET /obs/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; parent obs returned with groupMembers populated and each child referencing the parent obsGroup

Title: Start a visit via POST /visit and stop it via POST /visit/{uuid}
Preconditions: An existing patient with no open visit; a visit type and location configured
Test_Steps: 1. POST /visit with patient, visitType, location and startDatetime | 2. POST /visit/{uuid} with stopDatetime | 3. GET /visit/{uuid}
Expected_Result: First POST returns 201 with active visit (no stopDatetime); second POST sets stopDatetime; GET shows the visit as closed

Title: Reject a second overlapping active visit for the same patient
Preconditions: A patient who already has one active (open) visit
Test_Steps: 1. POST /visit for the same patient with no stopDatetime | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; an overlapping/concurrent active visit is not allowed; second visit not created

Title: Stop datetime before start datetime is rejected
Preconditions: An open visit started at 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. POST /visit/{uuid} with stopDatetime=09:00 | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; stop must be at or after start; visit remains open and unchanged

Title: Query active visits with includeInactive=false
Preconditions: At least one open and one closed visit exist for a patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /visit?patient={uuid}&includeInactive=false | 2. GET /visit?patient={uuid}&includeInactive=true
Expected_Result: With includeInactive=false only the open visit is returned; with true both open and closed visits are returned

Title: Create a drug order via POST /order with required dosing fields
Preconditions: An active encounter, an orderable drug concept, a careSetting and an ordering provider
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/order with type=drugorder, drug, dose, doseUnits, frequency, route and orderer | 2. GET /order/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; order created with action=NEW, a generated orderNumber and all dosing fields persisted

Title: Reject drug order missing dose when dosingType requires it (400)
Preconditions: An active encounter and orderable drug
Test_Steps: 1. POST /order drugorder with simple dosing but no dose | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation message naming the missing dose field; order not created

Title: Revise an order creates a new order with action=REVISE referencing prior
Preconditions: An existing active drug order
Test_Steps: 1. POST /order with action=REVISE, previousOrder set and a changed dose | 2. GET the new order and the previous order
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; new order has action=REVISE and previousOrder pointing to the original; original order is stopped/dateStopped set

Title: Discontinue an order via action=DISCONTINUE
Preconditions: An active order
Test_Steps: 1. POST /order with action=DISCONTINUE and previousOrder | 2. GET the original order
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; a discontinuation order is created and the original order is marked stopped; subsequent active-order queries exclude it

Title: Editing an existing order via POST /order/{uuid} is forbidden
Preconditions: An existing order
Test_Steps: 1. POST /order/{uuid} attempting to mutate fields directly | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: Request is rejected (HTTP 400); orders are immutable and may only be REVISEd or DISCONTINUEd, never edited in place

Title: Pairwise: order action x careSetting x urgency combinations
Preconditions: Configured INPATIENT and OUTPATIENT care settings
Test_Steps: 1. POST distinct orders across action {NEW,REVISE,DISCONTINUE} x careSetting {INPATIENT,OUTPATIENT} x urgency {ROUTINE,STAT,ON_SCHEDULED_DATE} | 2. Validate each accepted/rejected per rules
Expected_Result: Each valid combination is accepted with correct semantics; invalid combinations (e.g. DISCONTINUE without previousOrder) are rejected with 400

Title: Create a numeric concept with units and range via POST /concept
Preconditions: Admin privileges; concept datatype Numeric and a concept class available
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/concept with datatype Numeric, names and conceptClass | 2. POST /concept/{uuid} to set hiNormal/lowNormal/units | 3. GET /concept/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; concept created and the numeric detail subresource returns the configured units and reference range

Title: Search concepts by name with locale and class filter
Preconditions: Concepts exist in en and another locale
Test_Steps: 1. GET /concept?q=weight&locale=en | 2. GET /concept?q=weight&conceptClass={uuid}
Expected_Result: Results match the search term in the requested locale; class filter narrows results to the specified concept class

Title: Retire a concept and verify it is excluded from default search
Preconditions: An existing non-retired concept not referenced as an answer
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /concept/{uuid}?reason=Deprecated | 2. GET /concept?q={name} | 3. GET /concept/{uuid}?includeAll=true
Expected_Result: DELETE returns 204; default search no longer returns the concept; includeAll=true shows retired=true with retireReason

Title: Map a concept to a source/code via /concept/{uuid}/mapping
Preconditions: A concept and a configured concept source (e.g. SNOMED CT)
Test_Steps: 1. POST /concept/{uuid}/mapping with conceptReferenceTerm and mapType=SAME-AS | 2. GET /concept/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; the mapping appears with the correct source, code and SAME-AS map type for downstream terminology binding

Title: Create a provider linked to a person via POST /provider
Preconditions: An existing person and provider privileges
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/provider with person and identifier | 2. GET /provider/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; provider created and linked to the person; identifier persisted; retired=false

Title: Search providers by name/identifier and page results
Preconditions: Multiple providers exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /provider?q=PROV&limit=1 | 2. Request page 2 via startIndex=1
Expected_Result: Matching providers returned one per page; paging links present; total reflects all matches

Title: Retire a provider blocks new assignment but preserves history
Preconditions: A provider with at least one historical encounter
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /provider/{uuid}?reason=Left | 2. Attempt to create an encounter with the retired provider | 3. GET historical encounter
Expected_Result: Provider retired=true; assigning the retired provider to a new encounter is rejected or warned; historical encounters still resolve the provider display

Title: Create a location with tags via POST /location
Preconditions: Admin privileges; location tags configured
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/location with name and tags | 2. GET /location/{uuid}?v=full
Expected_Result: HTTP 201; location created with the assigned tags returned in full representation

Title: Filter locations by tag via /location?tag=
Preconditions: Locations with and without the Login Location tag
Test_Steps: 1. GET /location?tag=Login Location | 2. Inspect results
Expected_Result: Only locations carrying the Login Location tag are returned; untagged locations are excluded

Title: Retire a location referenced by an active visit is constrained
Preconditions: A location used as the location of an open visit
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /location/{uuid}?reason=Closed | 2. GET open visit | 3. Inspect impact
Expected_Result: Location retire succeeds but the existing visit still references it for history; new visits cannot select the retired location

Title: startIndex beyond total returns empty results not an error
Preconditions: A resource collection with a known small total (e.g. 3 patients matching)
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=Test&limit=10&startIndex=1000 | 2. Inspect results and total
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with an empty results array and the correct totalCount; no error is thrown

Title: limit=0 boundary returns zero results with valid total
Preconditions: A resource collection with several matches
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=Test&limit=0 | 2. Inspect results
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; results array empty; totalCount still reports the true number of matches (limit governs page size only)

Title: Negative limit is rejected or coerced safely
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=Test&limit=-5 | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: Either HTTP 400 with a validation message or limit is safely coerced to the default; the server never returns the full unpaged set unexpectedly

Title: Search term shorter than minimum length is rejected
Preconditions: Default search minimum character setting in effect
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=a | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 (or 200 with empty results) per the configured minimum search length; no expensive unbounded scan is performed

Title: includeAll=true exposes voided records to authorized users only
Preconditions: A voided patient and two users: one with and one without data-management privilege
Test_Steps: 1. As privileged user GET /patient/{uuid}?includeAll=true | 2. As basic user repeat
Expected_Result: Privileged user sees voided=true record; basic user receives 404 or is denied; voided data is not exposed without authorization

Title: Unauthenticated request returns 401 with no PHI
Preconditions: No valid session cookie or Basic credentials
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} without authentication | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; response body contains no patient PHI, only an authentication error

Title: Authenticated but unprivileged request returns 403
Preconditions: A user lacking Get Patients / manage privileges
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the restricted user | 2. GET a protected resource (e.g. /user) | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden; the action is denied and no protected data is returned

Title: Nonexistent uuid returns 404
Preconditions: Valid session
Expected_Result: HTTP 404 Not Found with a clear error message; no stack trace or internal path leaked

Title: Malformed JSON body returns 400 not 500
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. POST /patient with a truncated/invalid JSON body | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a parse error; the server does not return 500 or expose a stack trace

Title: Unsupported HTTP method returns 405
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. Send PUT /ws/rest/v1/patient (collection) | 2. Inspect status and Allow header
Expected_Result: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed with an Allow header listing permitted methods

Title: Accept application/json returns JSON; unsupported type returns 406
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient/{uuid} with Accept: application/json | 2. Repeat with Accept: application/xml | 3. Repeat with Accept: text/csv
Expected_Result: JSON request returns 200 application/json; unsupported media types return 406 Not Acceptable (or the documented supported set), never silently mis-typed content

Title: POST with wrong Content-Type returns 415
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. POST /patient with body but Content-Type: text/plain | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type; the JSON body is not parsed under the wrong content type

Title: Lost-update: two clients update same person concurrently
Preconditions: An existing person and two sessions reading the same version
Test_Steps: 1. Both clients GET /person/{uuid} | 2. Client A POST a gender change | 3. Client B POST an address change using its stale snapshot | 4. GET final state
Expected_Result: Last write does not silently clobber unrelated fields; either both changes merge correctly or the stale write is rejected; no field is lost without detection

Title: Conditional GET with If-None-Match / ETag returns 304 when unchanged
Preconditions: A resource that returns an ETag (or Last-Modified) header
Test_Steps: 1. GET resource and capture ETag | 2. GET again with If-None-Match set to that ETag | 3. Modify resource | 4. GET with the old If-None-Match
Expected_Result: If supported, step 2 returns 304 Not Modified with no body; after modification step 4 returns 200 with the new representation and a new ETag

Title: Conditional update with If-Match precondition fails on stale ETag (412)
Preconditions: A resource supporting optimistic concurrency via ETag/If-Match
Test_Steps: 1. GET resource and capture ETag | 2. Update the resource out-of-band | 3. POST update with the original (now stale) If-Match | 4. Inspect status
Expected_Result: Update is rejected with HTTP 412 Precondition Failed; the stale client is forced to refetch, preventing a lost update

Title: SQL injection in q parameter is neutralized
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=' OR '1'='1 | 2. GET /patient?q=test'; DROP TABLE person;-- | 3. Inspect results and DB integrity
Test_Data: q='' OR ''1''=''1 ; q with DROP TABLE
Expected_Result: Inputs are parameterized/escaped; no unintended rows returned and no schema change; query is treated as a literal search term

Title: Stored XSS payload in person name is not executed on read
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. POST /person with givenName containing a script payload | 2. GET /person/{uuid} | 3. Inspect raw JSON
Expected_Result: The payload is stored/returned as data only; the API does not render it; consuming clients receive properly encoded JSON with no executable markup interpreted server-side

Title: Direct object reference to another patient is access-controlled
Preconditions: A user whose role restricts patients to one location; a patient in another location
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the restricted user | 2. GET /patient/{otherLocationPatientUuid} | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: Access is denied (403 or 404) per location-based access control; the user cannot read a patient outside their authorized scope (no IDOR)

Title: Session fixation: logout invalidates the session token
Preconditions: An authenticated session with a known JSESSIONID
Test_Steps: 1. GET /session to confirm authenticated | 2. DELETE /session (logout) | 3. Reuse the old token on GET /patient/{uuid}
Expected_Result: After logout the old token is invalid; reuse returns 401; no PHI is served on the invalidated session

Title: Rate limiting / brute-force protection on /session
Preconditions: A known username
Test_Steps: 1. POST repeated Basic auth attempts with wrong passwords | 2. Observe lockout/throttle behavior
Expected_Result: After a threshold the account is locked or requests throttled; no credential enumeration via differing error messages

Title: Voided record retains row with audit columns set
Preconditions: DB read access; a patient voided via API
Test_Steps: 1. Void patient via DELETE | 2. Query patient table directly for the uuid
Expected_Result: Row persists with voided=1, voided_by, date_voided and void_reason populated; soft-delete semantics confirmed at the data layer

Title: Obs value stored in correct typed column per datatype
Preconditions: DB read access; numeric, coded and text obs created via API
Test_Steps: 1. Create obs of each datatype | 2. Query obs table for value_numeric, value_coded, value_text
Expected_Result: Numeric value in value_numeric, coded answer in value_coded, free text in value_text; no type is stored in the wrong column

Title: Referential integrity: encounter rows reference valid patient and visit
Preconditions: DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Create encounter via API | 2. Query encounter table foreign keys patient_id and visit_id
Expected_Result: Foreign keys resolve to existing, non-purged patient and visit rows; no orphaned encounter is created

Title: Read a FHIR R4 Patient via /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id}
Preconditions: A patient exists; FHIR2 module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{uuid} with Accept: application/fhir+json | 2. Validate the resource
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; a valid FHIR R4 Patient resourceType with id, name, gender and birthDate conforming to the R4 schema

Title: FHIR Patient search by identifier returns a Bundle
Preconditions: A patient with a known identifier; FHIR2 enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?identifier=1000X | 2. Validate the response
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; a searchset Bundle with total and entry array; the matching Patient is present with correct identifier system and value

Title: FHIR read of unknown id returns OperationOutcome 404
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/nonexistent | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: HTTP 404 with a FHIR OperationOutcome resource describing the not-found error (standards-conformant error envelope)

Title: FHIR Observation maps OpenMRS obs with LOINC code and value
Preconditions: An obs on a LOINC-mapped concept; FHIR2 enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Observation/{uuid} | 2. Validate code and value
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Observation has code.coding with the LOINC system URI and code, a valueQuantity (or valueCodeableConcept) and a subject reference to the Patient

Title: FHIR _count and pagination links on a search Bundle
Preconditions: Several FHIR Patients exist; FHIR2 enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient?_count=2 | 2. Follow the Bundle link rel=next
Expected_Result: Each Bundle page contains at most 2 entries and includes self and next links; following next yields the subsequent non-overlapping page

Title: Create a FHIR Patient via POST returns 201 with Location
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; write privileges
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with a valid R4 Patient body | 2. Inspect status, Location header and body
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 Created with a Location header pointing to the new resource and the persisted Patient echoed with a server-assigned id

Title: FHIR conditional create with If-None-Exist avoids duplicate
Preconditions: A FHIR Patient with identifier 1000X already exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient with If-None-Exist: identifier=1000X | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: If supported, server returns 200 referencing the existing resource rather than creating a duplicate; otherwise documents the behavior; no duplicate Patient is created

Title: Submit an HL7 v2 ADT^A04 message via the HL7 endpoint
Preconditions: HL7 inbound processing enabled; an identifier source mapped
Test_Steps: 1. POST an ADT^A04 (register patient) message to the HL7 inbound queue | 2. Process the queue | 3. GET the resulting patient
Test_Data: ADT^A04 with PID containing name, DOB, MRN
Expected_Result: Message is accepted and processed without error; a patient is created/updated reflecting the PID demographics; an ACK is generated

Title: Malformed HL7 message is queued as an error with NAK
Preconditions: HL7 inbound processing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST an HL7 message missing the required MSH-9 message type | 2. Process the queue | 3. Inspect the error queue
Expected_Result: Message fails processing and lands in the HL7 error queue with a descriptive error; a NAK/AR acknowledgment is produced; no partial patient data is committed

Title: ORU^R01 result message creates observations under an encounter
Preconditions: HL7 enabled; concepts mapped to OBX codes; an existing patient
Test_Steps: 1. POST an ORU^R01 with OBX segments | 2. Process the queue | 3. GET the patient encounters
Test_Data: ORU^R01 with two OBX numeric results
Expected_Result: An encounter with the OBX-derived observations is created and linked to the correct patient; obs values and units match the OBX fields

Title: totalCount only returned when totalCount=true requested
Preconditions: A collection with many matches
Test_Steps: 1. GET /patient?q=Test&limit=5 | 2. GET /patient?q=Test&limit=5&totalCount=true
Expected_Result: Without the flag the response may omit totalCount for performance; with totalCount=true the accurate grand total is returned alongside the page

Title: Stable ordering across pages prevents row skips/duplicates
Preconditions: A collection larger than two pages with a deterministic sort
Test_Steps: 1. Page through the entire collection in limit-sized chunks | 2. Collect all uuids | 3. Check for gaps/dupes
Expected_Result: The concatenation of all pages contains every uuid exactly once with no duplicates or omissions; ordering is stable between requests

Title: Explore unexpected behavior with deeply nested custom representations
Preconditions: An existing patient with rich subresources
Test_Steps: 1. Request progressively deeper v=custom expansions across person, identifiers, attributes and auditInfo | 2. Observe performance and correctness | 3. Note any field that fails to expand
Expected_Result: Deep expansions return correct nested data without server errors; any unsupported nesting is reported clearly rather than returning partial/garbled JSON; response time stays reasonable

Title: Probe timezone handling of encounterDatetime round-trip
Preconditions: An encounter created with an explicit timezone offset
Test_Steps: 1. POST /encounter with encounterDatetime including a +05:30 offset | 2. GET the encounter from clients in different timezones | 3. Compare instants
Expected_Result: The stored instant is unambiguous and round-trips to the same UTC moment regardless of client timezone; no off-by-one-day shift in the encounter date

Title: REST error payloads are machine-parseable for assistive client tooling
Preconditions: Valid session
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger 400, 404 and 403 errors | 2. Inspect each error body structure
Expected_Result: Each error returns a consistent JSON structure (error.message, error.code/detail) enabling assistive and automated clients to surface meaningful, localizable messages rather than opaque HTML

Title: Every create/update/void writes audit metadata retrievable via v=full
Preconditions: Privileges to view audit info
Test_Steps: 1. Create then update then void a person via API | 2. GET /person/{uuid}?v=full at each step | 3. Inspect auditInfo
Expected_Result: auditInfo reports creator+dateCreated on create, changedBy+dateChanged on update, and voidedBy+dateVoided+voidReason on void; an immutable audit trail is maintained for compliance
